changeSet.author: hdim
changeSet.comment: Seed email sequences for GTM launch outreach campaigns. Three sequences: Investor Introduction (5-day), Quality Leader Outreach (7-day), Network Reconnect (3-day). Designed for personalized, high-touch outreach with clear value propositions.

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM email_sequences WHERE tenant_id = '11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111'

column.name: name
column.value: Investor Introduction

column.name: description
column.value: 5-day compressed sequence for investor outreach. Personal intro with deck, follow-up engagement, content share, and meeting ask.

column.name: subject
column.value: HDIM - Healthcare Quality Measure Platform

column.name: preview_text
column.value: Building the infrastructure layer for value-based care

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

I'm reaching out because I'm building something I think you'll find interesting - HDIM (HealthData-in-Motion), an enterprise healthcare interoperability platform focused on clinical quality measures and HEDIS reporting.

The problem we're solving:

  • Healthcare organizations spend $2.1B annually on quality measure evaluation
  • Current solutions are fragmented, manual, and error-prone
  • Value-based care contracts require accurate, timely quality reporting

What we've built:

  • FHIR R4 native platform with CQL evaluation engine
  • Real-time care gap detection and risk stratification
  • 50+ microservices, production-ready architecture
  • Targeting HIEs, ACOs, and health plans

I've attached our deck with more details on market size, competitive landscape, and our go-to-market strategy.

View Pitch Deck

Would love to share more about what we're building and get your thoughts.

Best,
Aaron
Founder, HDIM

Unsubscribe from future emails

Hi {{firstName}},
I'm reaching out because I'm building something I think you'll find interesting - HDIM (HealthData-in-Motion), an enterprise healthcare interoperability platform focused on clinical quality measures and HEDIS reporting.
The problem we're solving: - Healthcare organizations spend $2.1B annually on quality measure evaluation - Current solutions are fragmented, manual, and error-prone - Value-based care contracts require accurate, timely quality reporting
What we've built: - FHIR R4 native platform with CQL evaluation engine - Real-time care gap detection and risk stratification - 50+ microservices, production-ready architecture - Targeting HIEs, ACOs, and health plans
I've attached our deck with more details on market size, competitive landscape, and our go-to-market strategy.
View Pitch Deck: {{deckLink}}
Would love to share more about what we're building and get your thoughts.
Best, Aaron Founder, HDIM
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}

column.name: subject
column.value: Quick follow-up on HDIM

column.name: preview_text
column.value: Wanted to make sure this didn't get buried

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

Just wanted to follow up on my note yesterday about HDIM.

I know inboxes get crowded, so here's the quick version:

We're building the quality measurement layer for value-based care.

Think of us as the Snowflake for healthcare quality data - aggregating clinical data from multiple EHRs and providing real-time quality measure evaluation.

The deck is here if you'd like to take a look: HDIM Pitch Deck

Happy to jump on a quick call if you have any questions.

Aaron

Unsubscribe

Hi {{firstName}},
Just wanted to follow up on my note yesterday about HDIM.
I know inboxes get crowded, so here's the quick version:
We're building the quality measurement layer for value-based care.
Think of us as the Snowflake for healthcare quality data - aggregating clinical data from multiple EHRs and providing real-time quality measure evaluation.
The deck is here if you'd like to take a look: {{deckLink}}
Happy to jump on a quick call if you have any questions.
Aaron
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}

column.name: subject
column.value: Healthcare quality reporting trends

column.name: preview_text
column.value: Some interesting market data I thought you'd appreciate

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

I came across some data I thought you might find interesting given the healthcare focus:

The shift to value-based care is accelerating:

  • CMS aims for 100% of Medicare beneficiaries in accountable care by 2030
  • Medicare Advantage now covers 50%+ of eligible beneficiaries
  • Star ratings directly impact plan revenue ($billions at stake)

Yet quality infrastructure is lagging:

  • Most organizations still rely on annual, retrospective measure calculation
  • Care gaps are identified months after the intervention window closes
  • No real-time visibility into quality performance

This is exactly the gap HDIM is addressing - real-time quality measurement that enables proactive care gap closure.

Would love to discuss how we're approaching this if you're interested.

Aaron

Unsubscribe

Hi {{firstName}},
I came across some data I thought you might find interesting given the healthcare focus:
The shift to value-based care is accelerating: - CMS aims for 100% of Medicare beneficiaries in accountable care by 2030 - Medicare Advantage now covers 50%+ of eligible beneficiaries - Star ratings directly impact plan revenue ($billions at stake)
Yet quality infrastructure is lagging: - Most organizations still rely on annual, retrospective measure calculation - Care gaps are identified months after the intervention window closes - No real-time visibility into quality performance
This is exactly the gap HDIM is addressing - real-time quality measurement that enables proactive care gap closure.
Would love to discuss how we're approaching this if you're interested.
Aaron
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}

column.name: subject
column.value: 15 minutes to discuss HDIM?

column.name: preview_text
column.value: Would love to walk you through what we're building

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

I'll keep this short - I've shared a lot about HDIM over the past few days, but nothing beats a quick conversation.

15 minutes to walk through:

  • Live demo of the platform
  • Our GTM strategy and early customer conversations
  • The round we're putting together

Are you available this week or next for a quick call?

Book a Time

If HDIM isn't a fit for your portfolio, I'd also appreciate any introductions to investors who focus on healthcare infrastructure.

Thanks,
Aaron

Unsubscribe

Hi {{firstName}},
I'll keep this short - I've shared a lot about HDIM over the past few days, but nothing beats a quick conversation.
15 minutes to walk through: - Live demo of the platform - Our GTM strategy and early customer conversations - The round we're putting together
Are you available this week or next for a quick call?
Book a Time: {{calendarLink}}
If HDIM isn't a fit for your portfolio, I'd also appreciate any introductions to investors who focus on healthcare infrastructure.
Thanks, Aaron
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}

column.name: name
column.value: Quality Leader Outreach

column.name: description
column.value: 7-day sequence for quality leaders at ACOs, HIEs, and health plans. Pain-focused messaging with ROI emphasis.

column.name: subject
column.value: Simplify HEDIS reporting for {{company}}

column.name: preview_text
column.value: Real-time quality measures without the spreadsheet chaos

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

I noticed {{company}} is managing quality reporting across multiple data sources - that's a challenge I hear about constantly from quality leaders.

The common pain points:

  • Annual HEDIS season is a fire drill
  • Care gaps are identified too late for intervention
  • Reconciling data from multiple EHRs is manual and error-prone
  • Star ratings and VBC contracts depend on numbers you can't trust

What if you could:

  • See real-time quality measure performance across all data sources
  • Identify care gaps while there's still time to close them
  • Generate HEDIS-compliant reports automatically
  • Trust your quality data year-round, not just at measurement time

I'm building HDIM specifically for organizations like {{company}}. I'd love to show you a quick demo.

See a Demo

Best,
Aaron
Founder, HDIM

Unsubscribe from future emails

Hi {{firstName}},
I noticed {{company}} is managing quality reporting across multiple data sources - that's a challenge I hear about constantly from quality leaders.
The common pain points: - Annual HEDIS season is a fire drill - Care gaps are identified too late for intervention - Reconciling data from multiple EHRs is manual and error-prone - Star ratings and VBC contracts depend on numbers you can't trust
What if you could: - See real-time quality measure performance across all data sources - Identify care gaps while there's still time to close them - Generate HEDIS-compliant reports automatically - Trust your quality data year-round, not just at measurement time
I'm building HDIM specifically for organizations like {{company}}. I'd love to show you a quick demo.
See a Demo: {{demoLink}}
Best, Aaron Founder, HDIM
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}

column.name: subject
column.value: Star ratings and quality gaps

column.name: preview_text
column.value: The hidden cost of delayed quality measurement

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

Following up on my note about HDIM - I wanted to share a specific example of what we're solving.

The Star Rating Math:

  • A 0.5 star improvement can mean $500+ per member per year in bonus payments
  • For a 100K member plan, that's $50M annually
  • Most of that gap comes down to care gap closure rates on HEDIS measures

The Problem:

By the time you identify a care gap in traditional HEDIS workflows, the measurement year is often over. The patient who needed a diabetic eye exam in Q2 gets flagged in Q4 - too late.

Our Approach:

HDIM calculates quality measures in real-time against live clinical data. You see the gap when there's still time to close it.

Would you have 15 minutes to see how this could work for {{company}}?

Aaron

Unsubscribe

Hi {{firstName}},
Following up on my note about HDIM - I wanted to share a specific example of what we're solving.
The Star Rating Math: - A 0.5 star improvement can mean $500+ per member per year in bonus payments - For a 100K member plan, that's $50M annually - Most of that gap comes down to care gap closure rates on HEDIS measures
The Problem: By the time you identify a care gap in traditional HEDIS workflows, the measurement year is often over. The patient who needed a diabetic eye exam in Q2 gets flagged in Q4 - too late.
Our Approach: HDIM calculates quality measures in real-time against live clinical data. You see the gap when there's still time to close it.
Would you have 15 minutes to see how this could work for {{company}}?
Aaron
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}

column.name: subject
column.value: Quick question about your quality measures

column.name: preview_text
column.value: Curious how {{company}} handles this challenge

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

Quick question - when you're running quality measure calculations at {{company}}, how do you handle the data reconciliation challenge?

I ask because every quality leader I talk to mentions this as a major pain point:

  • Patient shows up in EHR A with one MRN
  • Same patient in claims data with a different identifier
  • Lab results come from a third system
  • Reconciling these for a single HEDIS measure is a nightmare

We've built HDIM's architecture specifically to solve this - unified patient identity with FHIR R4 data normalization across all sources.

Curious to hear how you're currently approaching this. Even if HDIM isn't a fit, I'd love to learn from your experience.

Aaron

Unsubscribe

Hi {{firstName}},
Quick question - when you're running quality measure calculations at {{company}}, how do you handle the data reconciliation challenge?
I ask because every quality leader I talk to mentions this as a major pain point: - Patient shows up in EHR A with one MRN - Same patient in claims data with a different identifier - Lab results come from a third system - Reconciling these for a single HEDIS measure is a nightmare
We've built HDIM's architecture specifically to solve this - unified patient identity with FHIR R4 data normalization across all sources.
Curious to hear how you're currently approaching this. Even if HDIM isn't a fit, I'd love to learn from your experience.
Aaron
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}

column.name: subject
column.value: How HDIM saves 40% on quality reporting

column.name: preview_text
column.value: Real numbers from organizations like yours

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

I wanted to share some concrete numbers on the ROI we're seeing with HDIM:

Time Savings:

  • 40% reduction in HEDIS reporting effort
  • From quarterly batch calculations to real-time continuous measurement
  • Automated data extraction replaces manual chart review

Quality Improvement:

  • 15-20% improvement in care gap closure rates
  • Gaps identified 60-90 days earlier in the measurement cycle
  • Actionable worklists for care management teams

Revenue Impact:

  • Star rating improvements translate directly to bonus payments
  • VBC contract performance tied to quality metrics
  • Avoid penalties from missed quality thresholds

Happy to walk through a custom ROI analysis for {{company}} if you're interested.

Aaron

Unsubscribe

Hi {{firstName}},
I wanted to share some concrete numbers on the ROI we're seeing with HDIM:
Time Savings: - 40% reduction in HEDIS reporting effort - From quarterly batch calculations to real-time continuous measurement - Automated data extraction replaces manual chart review
Quality Improvement: - 15-20% improvement in care gap closure rates - Gaps identified 60-90 days earlier in the measurement cycle - Actionable worklists for care management teams
Revenue Impact: - Star rating improvements translate directly to bonus payments - VBC contract performance tied to quality metrics - Avoid penalties from missed quality thresholds
Happy to walk through a custom ROI analysis for {{company}} if you're interested.
Aaron
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}

column.name: subject
column.value: 15 minutes to see HDIM in action?

column.name: preview_text
column.value: Live demo tailored to {{company}}'s needs

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

I've sent a few notes about HDIM over the past week - rather than keep filling your inbox, I'd love to show you the platform directly.

In 15 minutes, I can walk you through:

  • Real-time HEDIS measure calculation
  • Care gap identification and prioritization
  • Multi-source data integration (EHRs, claims, labs)
  • How this could work for {{company}} specifically

Book a Demo

If the timing isn't right, no worries - just let me know and I'll check back in a few months.

Thanks,
Aaron

Unsubscribe

Hi {{firstName}},
I've sent a few notes about HDIM over the past week - rather than keep filling your inbox, I'd love to show you the platform directly.
In 15 minutes, I can walk you through: - Real-time HEDIS measure calculation - Care gap identification and prioritization - Multi-source data integration (EHRs, claims, labs) - How this could work for {{company}} specifically
Book a Demo: {{calendarLink}}
If the timing isn't right, no worries - just let me know and I'll check back in a few months.
Thanks, Aaron
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}

column.name: name
column.value: Network Reconnect

column.name: description
column.value: 3-day sequence for reconnecting with professional network. Personal, authentic tone focused on sharing what you've been working on.

column.name: subject
column.value: Catching up - and something new

column.name: preview_text
column.value: It's been a while - wanted to share what I've been working on

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

It's been a while since we've connected - I hope you're doing well!

I wanted to reach out because I've been heads-down building something new, and you came to mind as someone who might find it interesting (or know others who would).

I'm building HDIM - a healthcare interoperability platform focused on clinical quality measures. Think of it as the infrastructure layer for value-based care.

The short version:

  • Real-time HEDIS measure calculation
  • Care gap detection across multiple EHRs
  • FHIR R4 native architecture
  • Targeting HIEs, ACOs, and health plans

I'd love to catch up and hear what you've been working on too. Coffee (virtual or otherwise) sometime?

Best,
Aaron

Unsubscribe

Hi {{firstName}},
It's been a while since we've connected - I hope you're doing well!
I wanted to reach out because I've been heads-down building something new, and you came to mind as someone who might find it interesting (or know others who would).
I'm building HDIM - a healthcare interoperability platform focused on clinical quality measures. Think of it as the infrastructure layer for value-based care.
The short version: - Real-time HEDIS measure calculation - Care gap detection across multiple EHRs - FHIR R4 native architecture - Targeting HIEs, ACOs, and health plans
I'd love to catch up and hear what you've been working on too. Coffee (virtual or otherwise) sometime?
Best, Aaron
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}

column.name: subject
column.value: What I've been working on

column.name: preview_text
column.value: A quick look at HDIM

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

Following up on my note - I realized I was pretty brief about what I'm actually building. Here's a bit more context:

The Problem I'm Solving:

Healthcare organizations (think HIEs, ACOs, health plans) spend billions on quality measure reporting - HEDIS, Star ratings, CMS requirements. Most of it is still done with manual chart review, spreadsheets, and annual batch calculations.

What HDIM Does:

We provide real-time clinical quality measure evaluation. Plug in your data sources (EHRs, claims, labs), and we continuously calculate measures and identify care gaps - while there's still time to close them.

Where We Are:

  • 50+ microservices in production-ready architecture
  • FHIR R4 native with CQL evaluation engine
  • Starting customer conversations with HIEs from my previous work

If you know anyone in healthcare quality, population health, or health IT who might be interested, I'd really appreciate an introduction.

And genuinely - I'd love to hear what you're up to as well.

Aaron

Unsubscribe

Hi {{firstName}},
Following up on my note - I realized I was pretty brief about what I'm actually building. Here's a bit more context:
The Problem I'm Solving: Healthcare organizations (think HIEs, ACOs, health plans) spend billions on quality measure reporting - HEDIS, Star ratings, CMS requirements. Most of it is still done with manual chart review, spreadsheets, and annual batch calculations.
What HDIM Does: We provide real-time clinical quality measure evaluation. Plug in your data sources (EHRs, claims, labs), and we continuously calculate measures and identify care gaps - while there's still time to close them.
Where We Are: - 50+ microservices in production-ready architecture - FHIR R4 native with CQL evaluation engine - Starting customer conversations with HIEs from my previous work
If you know anyone in healthcare quality, population health, or health IT who might be interested, I'd really appreciate an introduction.
And genuinely - I'd love to hear what you're up to as well.
Aaron
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}

column.name: subject
column.value: Would love your thoughts

column.name: preview_text
column.value: A quick ask - and genuine interest in reconnecting

insert.column:
Hi {{firstName}},

Last note from me (I promise!) - just wanted to make a direct ask:

Two things that would be super helpful:

1. Introductions
If you know anyone at HIEs, ACOs, health plans, or health systems who deals with quality measurement or HEDIS reporting, I'd love an intro. Even a "hey, talk to Aaron about what he's building" would mean a lot.

2. Your perspective
If you have 15-20 minutes, I'd genuinely value your thoughts on what I'm building. Sometimes an outside perspective catches things I'm too close to see.

Grab Time on My Calendar

Either way, I hope we can catch up soon. Let me know what you've been working on - always interested to hear.

Thanks,
Aaron

Unsubscribe

Hi {{firstName}},
Last note from me (I promise!) - just wanted to make a direct ask:
Two things that would be super helpful:
1. Introductions If you know anyone at HIEs, ACOs, health plans, or health systems who deals with quality measurement or HEDIS reporting, I'd love an intro. Even a "hey, talk to Aaron about what he's building" would mean a lot.
2. Your perspective If you have 15-20 minutes, I'd genuinely value your thoughts on what I'm building. Sometimes an outside perspective catches things I'm too close to see.
Grab Time on My Calendar: {{calendarLink}}
Either way, I hope we can catch up soon. Let me know what you've been working on - always interested to hear.
Thanks, Aaron
--- Unsubscribe: {{unsubscribeLink}}
